commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds OpenAI TTS parsing for `speed` and OpenAI speed aliases, forwards provider overrides through OpenAI telephony synthesis, adds regression tests, and adds a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +107, Docs +1. Total +127 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main's OpenAI provider parser returns `handled: false` for `speed`, while the lower OpenAI TTS request builder already serializes `speed` when an override exists. I did not run a live repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can preserve the useful OpenAI speed directive fix while rebasing current paths, aligning numeric parsing, and removing release-owned changelog churn.

target.title: fix(openai/tts): handle [[tts:speed]] directive in OpenAI speech provider (#12163)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `[[tts:speed=N]]` directives reached the TTS directive router but the OpenAI speech provider did not claim `speed`, so OpenAI provider overrides were never populated. ElevenLabs already handled the same directive family.
- **Why it matters:** OpenAI TTS users could not tune one reply's speech rate with model-emitted TTS directives, despite OpenAI's `/audio/speech` API already supporting `speed`.
- **What changed:** Added OpenAI-owned handling for `speed`, `openai_speed`, and `openaispeed`, gated by `allowVoiceSettings` and bounded to OpenAI's `0.25..4` range. Also keeps provider overrides flowing through OpenAI telephony synthesis so the same directive works for `mp3` and `pcm` request bodies.
- **What did NOT change:** No core directive-routing behavior, config shape, credential handling, or provider selection logic changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #12163
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: an OpenAI-selected `[[tts:speed=1.5]]` directive should become an OpenAI provider override and then serialize as `"speed": 1.5` in `/v1/audio/speech` requests.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node 22.22.0, production `parseTtsDirectives`, production OpenAI speech provider, and the real SSRF-guarded OpenAI fetch path pointed at a local HTTP capture server. The API key was fake/redacted; no live OpenAI request was sent.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '<local capture server + parseTtsDirectives + buildOpenAISpeechProvider>'` from the repo root. The script parsed `[[tts:speed=1.5]] hello speed directive`, passed the resulting OpenAI provider override into normal synthesis and telephony synthesis, and captured the production `/v1/audio/speech` request bodies.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "directive": {
    "cleanedText": "hello speed directive",
    "warnings": [],
    "providerOverrides": {
      "openai": {
        "speed": 1.5
      }
    }
  },
  "audioFileRequest": {
    "method": "POST",
    "url": "/v1/audio/speech",
    "body": {
      "model": "gpt-4o-mini-tts",
      "input": "hello speed directive",
      "voice": "alloy",
      "response_format": "mp3",
      "speed": 1.5
    }
  },
  "telephonyRequest": {
    "method": "POST",
    "url": "/v1/audio/speech",
    "body": {
      "model": "gpt-4o-mini-tts",
      "input": "hello telephony speed directive",
      "voice": "alloy",
      "response_format": "pcm",
      "speed": 1.5
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: `parseTtsDirectives` produced `providerOverrides.openai.speed === 1.5`; the captured normal OpenAI request body contained `"response_format": "mp3"` and `"speed": 1.5`; the captured telephony OpenAI request body contained `"response_format": "pcm"` and `"speed": 1.5`.
- What was not tested: a live OpenAI API request with a real key was not sent; the proof intentionally used a local HTTP capture server with a fake key to avoid exposing credentials while still exercising the production directive parser, provider, request-body builder, and SSRF-guarded fetch path.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** OpenAI `parseDirectiveToken` handled voice/model aliases only and returned `{ handled: false }` for `speed`. `parseTtsDirectives` only writes provider overrides when a provider parser returns `handled: true`, so OpenAI never received the speed override.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No OpenAI provider test covered preferred-provider speed directives, explicit OpenAI speed aliases, disabled `allowVoiceSettings`, invalid speed text, out-of-range speed, or telephony synthesis override forwarding.
- **Contributing context:** Generic directive routing is provider-first by design; provider-owned keys must opt in locally.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests:
  - `extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts`
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: OpenAI parses speed directives into provider overrides, rejects invalid values, respects `allowVoiceSettings`, and forwards provider overrides to telephony synthesis.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in provider-owned directive parsing plus provider override propagation, so focused provider and speech-core tests cover the actual contracts without broad channel/provider E2E setup.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `[[tts:speed=1.5]]` now works for OpenAI TTS when OpenAI is the selected/preferred provider.
- `[[tts:provider=openai speed=1.5]]` now applies when provider directives are allowed.
- Invalid text values warn; out-of-range numeric values throw through the existing `requireInRange` behavior.
- Disabled `allowVoiceSettings` still drops the directive.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts` -> 14 passed
- `pnpm test extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts -- -t "passes directive overrides to telephony synthesis providers"` -> 1 passed, 23 skipped
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json` -> `extensions`, `extensionTests`, `docs`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Provider speed ranges differ. **Mitigation:** keep parsing inside `extensions/openai` and use OpenAI's documented `0.25..4` range.
- **Risk:** Telephony path could drift from normal synthesis. **Mitigation:** add focused coverage that asserts telephony receives provider overrides.
target.author: stainlu

commands.reason: repair commands require an open issue or PR
